attributes.description: Alerts when lock waits exceed 10 requests/sec. High wait rate means queries routinely block on each other.
attributes.name: [SQL Server OTel] Lock wait rate high

investigation_guide.blob:
## SQL Server lock wait rate high

### What fired
Average `sqlserver.lock.wait.rate` exceeded 10/sec over the window.

### Why it matters
Lock waits indicate contention even when no timeouts have occurred. Sustained high wait rates correlate with blocked processes and throughput degradation.

### Triage
1. Profile top wait types.
2. Identify hot tables / indexes.

### Remediation
- Tune indexes / query plans.
- Use snapshot isolation where appropriate.

### Tuning
- 10/sec is a moderate starting point.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-sqlserverreceiver.otel-default
// Lock requests resulting in waits per second; high values mean queries blocking each other
| WHERE metrics.sqlserver.lock.wait.rate IS NOT NULL
| STATS avg_wait_rate = AVG(metrics.sqlserver.lock.wait.rate),
       instance_id = MAX(resource.attributes.service.instance.id)
  BY COALESCE(resource.attributes.service.instance.id, host.name, "unknown")
// Adjust threshold (requests/s) based on expected workload; 10 is a starting point
| WHERE avg_wait_rate > 10.0
| KEEP instance_id, avg_wait_rate
